packages.summary: On October 15, 2025, the UTSC Campus Affairs Committee received three information presentations on strategic priorities. Chief Administrative Officer Andrew Arifuzzaman presented campus priorities for 2025/2026 including implementing an activity-based budget model in academic units, addressing international student enrollment shifts (targeting 1,428 international undergraduate headcount by 2028 vs. 1,141 in 2023), building SAMIH-related health science programming across three faculties, operationalizing new facilities including the Myron and Berna Garron Health Sciences Complex, the Retail & Parking Commons, Indigenous House, and Toronto Zoo Conservation Campus, and implementing a Culture of Care initiative. The committee also received the Annual Report: Community Partnerships and Engagement, 2025, highlighting the Office of Community Partnerships & Engagement's transition to report directly to the Office of the Vice-President and Principal as of May 1, 2025, under director Kimberley Tull. Key initiatives included the Kona Project (funded by Connaught Foundation, involving 12 Black researchers and 40+ students), the Sophia Hilton Storytelling Fellowship ($32,000 total funding to eight students), Captivate digital storytelling competition (55 students engaged, 29 submissions), and SDGs@UofT providing $370,809 in Catalyst and Synthesis Grants plus $40,500 in student awards to 27 graduate and undergraduate students.
packages.gradRelevance: Graduate students benefit directly from multiple funding and research opportunities: SDGs@UofT awarded $40,500 in student awards to 22 graduate students across 9 faculties and 17 departments to advance research; the Sophia Hilton Storytelling Fellowship provides $32,000 total funding to students (primarily undergraduates but available to grad students); the Kona Project involves 40+ students including potential graduate researchers from U of T in Black health equity research; and the SDGs Scholars Academy includes 202 Affiliates and 12 Fellows supporting graduate-level transdisciplinary work. The activity-based budget model will affect graduate program funding allocations and departmental research measures. International student enrollment policy shifts (targeting 1,428 by 2028) may impact graduate program composition and TA/RA availability in departments.
keyItems: Activity-based budget model implementation: UTSC is establishing a new decentralized budget allocation system using activity units of measure (FCE, program enrollment, research measures) with principles emphasizing transparency, incentives for revenue generation, and flexibility for academic evolution across departments.

International student enrollment: Current headcount is 953 (2023), projecting to 1,428 by 2028; international fees comprise 17% of projected 2025-26 operating revenue (down from 22% in 2019-20), while domestic fees are 62% and operating grants 16%.

SAMIH (Myron and Berna Garron Health Sciences Complex) operationalization: Bringing together Temerty Faculty of Medicine, Lawrence Bloomberg Faculty of Nursing, and Leslie Dan Faculty of Pharmacy; defining daily operations and technology/support services.
Retail & Parking Commons operationalization: Implementing technology systems, negotiating with TPASC (Third Party Administrator/Service Contractor), and operationalizing new bookstore.
Indigenous House opening: Hiring new Director and conducting staffing activities to establish operations.
Toronto Zoo Conservation Campus: New shared facility with classrooms, labs, and research spaces becoming operational.

documents.title: Status Report on UTSC Campus Strategic Priorities
documents.summary: Document confirms the governance framework and timeline for UTSC's strategic priorities review. Presented by Vice-Principal Academic & Dean Karin Ruhlandt, Vice-President & Principal Linda Johnston, and Chief Administrative Officer Andrew Arifuzzaman. Establishes that UTSC Campus Affairs Committee will receive information on campus strategic priorities prior to administrative budget review (late Fall 2025), with Campus Operating Budget presentation following in Cycle 5. Notes previous presentation occurred October 16, 2024.
documents.gradRelevance: Establishes formal governance process affecting graduate program budgets and resource allocation through activity-based budgeting and departmental complement planning. Directly impacts graduate student funding streams and departmental research priorities through new budget model implementation.

documents.title: Campus Priorities for the 2025/2026 Fiscal Year (Presentation Slides)
documents.summary: Comprehensive presentation by Andrew Arifuzzaman outlining five strategic priorities: (1) implementing activity-based budget model emphasizing decentralized transparency and departmental innovation incentives; (2) managing international student enrollment shift with headcount targets of 1,428 by 2028 (vs. 953 in 2023), showing domestic fees at 62% and international fees at 17% of operating revenue; (3) building SAMIH health science programming integrating three faculties; (4) operationalizing new physical facilities (Garron Health Sciences Complex, Retail & Parking Commons, Indigenous House, Toronto Zoo Conservation Campus); (5) implementing Culture of Care based on Principal Linda Johnston's January 2025 Installation Address, emphasizing psychological safety, equity, and joy in work with 70% employee thriving when valued.
documents.gradRelevance: Directly impacts graduate students through budget model changes affecting departmental funding, international student enrollment affecting program composition and TA/RA demand, SAMIH expansion creating new graduate program opportunities in health sciences, and Culture of Care framework shaping workplace policies, mental health support, and research environment.

documents.title: Annual Report: Community Partnerships and Engagement, 2025
documents.summary: Annual report titled 'With Care' covering Community Partnerships & Engagement office activities across three themes: Research as Resistance, Pathways to Access, and Engagement with Purpose. Reports office structural change (CPE reports directly to Vice-President and Principal as of May 1, 2025) under director Kimberley Tull. Details major initiatives: Kona Project (Connaught Foundation-funded, 12 Black researchers, 12 community health leaders, 40+ students), Sophia Hilton Storytelling Fellowship ($32,000 to eight students over three years), Captivate digital storytelling competition (55 students, 29 submissions, PIE-funded), SDG Scholars Academy (12 Fellows, 202 Affiliates, $370,809 in grants), SDGs@UofT student awards ($40,500 to 27 students), Global Classroom for Democracy Innovation (400+ students across 10+ universities), and land-based learning partnerships. Emphasizes care as guiding principle aligned with strategic plan Inspiring Inclusive Excellence.
documents.gradRelevance: Critical for graduate students: SDGs@UofT awarded $40,500 in student awards specifically to 22 graduate students and 5 undergraduates across 9 faculties and 17 departments; distributed $370,809 in Catalyst and Synthesis Grants supporting graduate research; 9-member renewed Student Advisory Committee includes graduate representation. Kona Project involves graduate researchers. Global Classroom for Democracy Innovation provides experiential learning for graduate students. Sophia Hilton Fellowship and Captivate programs support graduate students in digital scholarship and storytelling.

packages.title: UTSC Campus Affairs Committee - October 15, 2025
packages.summary: At its October 15, 2025 meeting, the UTSC Campus Affairs Committee heard reports on five strategic priorities: transitioning to an activity-based budget model for academic units, addressing declining international student enrollment (applications from India down 70%, China significantly down), building the Scarborough Academy of Medicine and Integrated Health (SAMIH), operationalizing new facilities including the Myron and Bernard Garron Health Sciences Complex, Indigenous House, and Retail & Parking Commons, and implementing Culture of Care, Healthy Campus, and Inspiring Inclusive Excellence initiatives. The Committee also received the Community Partnerships and Engagement Annual Report highlighting 80+ campus-community connections since January 2025, 22+ events, and the 'A Day in the Life' program (14 iterations, 200+ youth participants, $23,000 RSM Foundation grant). Enrollment stands at 15,000 total students (11,000 domestic, 4,000 international); Life Sciences and Health Certificates are the largest growth area; the Department of Management exceeded 600 new students. One vacant graduate student seat on the Committee remains unfilled after a by-election.
packages.gradRelevance: Graduate students are directly affected by the activity-based budget model's impact on departmental resources and TA/RA hiring: biology labs are growing significantly and requiring more teaching assistants, though no specific TA stipend or funding changes are announced. The declining international student enrollment poses budget challenges that could affect graduate funding and support services. The new SAMIH complex will provide 'specialized technological supports and services' relevant to health sciences graduate programs. The Health & Wellness Centre's 'We're Not Strangers' event and 'Beyond the Brain' healthy campus initiative address graduate student wellbeing, and the new ESL courses offered in-house at lower cost than School of Continuing Studies may benefit international graduate students.
keyItems: Activity-based budget model for academic units to begin implementation: provides departmental chairs greater control and predictability, incentivizes revenue generation and cost savings; budget resource allocation to remain decentralized, flexible, transparent, with simplified reporting and expert oversight.

International student enrollment decline continuing: applications from India dropped 70%, applications from China significantly dropped; diversification of recruitment across many countries required but difficult to match previous large-country application numbers.

UTSC total enrollment reaches 15,000 students (highest in campus history): 11,000 domestic, 4,000 international; Fall 2025 target was 4,331 with domestic targets met but significant international shortfall.

Life Sciences and Health Certificate programs identified as largest growth area requiring additional technicians and teaching assistants; Department of Management enrolled record 600+ new students; social sciences and humanities departments recovered to previous average enrollment levels.

Community Partnerships and Engagement office moved to reporting directly to Principal Linda Johnston following administrative review; office facilitated 80+ campus-community connections and hosted 22+ events since January 2025.

'A Day in the Life' immersive outreach program (14 iterations over two years, 200+ youth from equity-deserving groups): received $23,000 RSM Foundation grant for expansion; partners include BGC East Scarborough, Visions of Science, Black Esteem, and local schools.

packages.title: UTSC Campus Affairs Committee - September 10, 2025
packages.summary: The UTSC Campus Affairs Committee met on September 10, 2025 to receive an overview of the Council on Student Services (CSS) Budget Process for 2025-26. The CSS budget process involved 70 students across 5 CSS meetings, 18 departmental advisory committee meetings, 2 finance advisory meetings, and 1 preliminary budget meeting. The presentation outlined CSS themes and the robust budgeting protocol governing student services allocations at UTSC. The committee approved the Report of the Previous Meeting (Report Number 63 from May 8, 2025) and scheduled the next meeting for Wednesday, October 15, 2025.
packages.gradRelevance: The CSS Budget Overview directly affects graduate students as the Council on Student Services allocates funding for student-facing programs and services. Graduate students are included in the 70 students involved in the CSS budget process, giving them voice in how student fees are spent on services, programs, and supports. However, the document provided does not specify which particular services, fee amounts, TA/RA compensation, or graduate-specific programs were discussed or proposed for 2025-26.
keyItems: CSS Budget Process Overview presented for 2025-26 with 70 students involved in consultation across multiple committee structures
CSS budget development included 5 CSS meetings, 18 departmental advisory committee meetings, 2 finance advisory meetings, and 1 preliminary budget meeting
Report of the Previous Meeting (Report Number 63, May 8, 2025) was approved by the committee

Presidential Assessors provided introductions and reports: Karin Ruhlandt (Vice-Principal Academic & Dean), Tim Tang (Dean, Student Experience & Wellbeing), and Andrew Arifuzzaman (Chief Administrative and Strategy Officer)
Next CAC meeting scheduled for Wednesday, October 15, 2025
CSS themes for 2025-26 were presented but specific content is not legible in the provided document
